name: Thea Andrade
story_relevance_note: Phoenix Union HSD is the largest urban high school district in Maricopa County, in the EPA serious PM10 non-attainment zone. Decisions about MERV 13 procurement and HVAC commissioning rest with the superintendent's office. Asking on the record whether the district has measured PM2.5 or commissioned its filter housings under ASHRAE 241.

name: Dr. Matthew Strom
story_relevance_note: Largest district in Arizona by enrollment. Confirmed superintendent since July 2025. Mesa is in the Maricopa serious PM10 non-attainment zone; what we want to know: did MPS verify housing seal performance, or only buy MERV 13 media?
personalization_hook: Saw your letter to the Mesa Public Schools community at the start of the 2025-26 school year.

name: Michael Kramer
role: Executive Director of Operations and Construction
story_relevance_note: Operations and Construction owns HVAC procurement and commissioning. He is the named accountable party for whether housings were sealed. Public records request target for HVAC service invoices (PRR exhibit in the investigation).
personalization_hook: Saw your request for the $1M waste recycling services contract with the city, approved by the Mesa school board.

name: Joseph Orrantia
story_relevance_note: Maintenance and Grounds is the operational unit running filter changes and HVAC service contracts. Day-to-day awareness of which units are bypassing media.

name: Dr. Scott A. Menzel
story_relevance_note: SUSD parent base is engaged on facilities; district has spent on HVAC upgrades. Asking whether SUSD has produced commissioning reports or PM2.5 readings to back hospital-grade claims.
personalization_hook: Saw your Facility Condition Assessment approved by the Governing Board in October 2024 to identify HVAC and other building systems needs for a 10-year Capital Plan.

name: Dr. Frank Narducci
story_relevance_note: CUSD is the third-largest AZ district. ESSER-funded HVAC upgrades documented during COVID; we want the duct-leakage and PM2.5 follow-up data.
personalization_hook: Saw your approval of the $265K contract for incoming Su.

name: Dr. Curtis Finch
story_relevance_note: Deer Valley sits in the north Maricopa PM10 zone. Long-tenured superintendent; he owns the procurement decisions on filter media for the district's 30+ campuses.
personalization_hook: Saw your Arizona School Administrators/Arizona School Boards Association All Arizona Su.

name: Dr. Cort Monroe
story_relevance_note: Smaller district but high parent education and engagement. Good for getting an early on-the-record answer on whether they've measured housing bypass.
personalization_hook: Saw your leadership on revitalizing CCUSD's strategic plan with community stakeholder input.

name: Dr. Troy Bales
story_relevance_note: PVUSD has campuses in PM10 zone. District has communicated proactively about indoor air during COVID; question is whether claims survive ASHRAE 241 housing scrutiny.
personalization_hook: Saw your announcement of retirement after 25 years in PVSchools, leaving a legacy of integrity and student focus.

name: Dr. Christine Busch
story_relevance_note: Tempe Elementary is a K-8 district. Younger lungs are more sensitive to PM2.5 (Sunyer 2015 showed cognitive deficits at school-relevant exposures). Pediatric-aged audience makes this district central to the story.

name: Dr. Kevin Mendivil
story_relevance_note: Tempe Union HSD adjacent. We already have Tempe career-school harvest in this campaign; this completes Tempe coverage at the public-school level.

name: Dr. Gabriel Trujillo
story_relevance_note: Largest southern Arizona district. Pima County has its own air quality particulars (PM2.5 from western dust events). Story has statewide reach if TUSD comments.
personalization_hook: Saw your leadership on the 23.8 MW solar project, Arizona's largest among K-12 districts, saving TUSD $1.1 million annually.

name: Sen. Hildy Angius
story_relevance_note: Chairs the Senate panel that would hear any school IAQ disclosure or HVAC commissioning legislation. Direct policy hook: should AZ require districts to publish PM2.5 readings and ASHRAE 241 housing tests?
personalization_hook: Saw your introduction of legislation with Rep. Gress to ban teacher strikes and protect classroom instruction following mass sick-outs.

name: Sen. Carine Werner
story_relevance_note: Vice chair of Senate Ed. Often paired comment opportunity with the chair when there's a specific policy ask on the table.
personalization_hook: Saw your motion in the Senate Education Committee to continue the Western Interstate Commission for Higher Education for 10 years until July 1, 2036.

name: Sen. David Farnsworth
story_relevance_note: Senior member of Senate Ed. Mesa-area district overlap with MPS, the largest district in the story.
personalization_hook: Saw your advancement of SB 1026 through the Senate Education Committee to cut red tape for alternative education programs.

name: Sen. Flavio Bravo
story_relevance_note: Minority member of Senate Ed. Phoenix Union HSD overlap. Likely friendly to disclosure policy framing.
personalization_hook: Saw your SB1299 on bleed control kits for school districts.

name: Sen. Eva Diaz
story_relevance_note: Minority Senate Ed. West Valley districts represented in her LD; likely receptive to a children's environmental health frame.
personalization_hook: Saw your sponsorship of **SB1327** on **Arizona online instruction cost study**.

name: Sen. Catherine Miranda
story_relevance_note: South Phoenix LD member; cumulative pollution burden + school IAQ framing fits her district base.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on homelessness and education solutions for Arizona families and children.

name: Rep. Matt Gress
story_relevance_note: Chairs the House Ed Committee. Any disclosure bill (require districts to publish PM2.5 + commissioning data) starts with him.
personalization_hook: Saw your school governance reform package, including HB2387 and HB2386 on su.

name: Rep. Nancy Gutierrez
story_relevance_note: Tucson Democrat on House Ed. Former educator; pediatric exposure framing reads strongly in her district.
personalization_hook: Saw your HB2506 on school funding inflation adjustment referred to the House Education Committee.

name: Rep. Brian Garcia
story_relevance_note: Tempe-area House Ed Democrat; direct district overlap with Tempe Elementary and Tempe Union, both in this list.
personalization_hook: Saw your co-sponsorship of House Bill 2281 establishing the Turquoise Alert system for missing Indigenous.

name: Andrew Arthur, MD, PhD, FAAP
story_relevance_note: Pediatrician, statewide asthma policy convener. The asthma + AZ child cognition + PM2.5 story sits squarely in the coalition's mandate.
personalization_hook: Saw your KXCI Tucson Interview on Asthma.

name: Rajeev Bhatia, MD
story_relevance_note: Division Chief of Pulmonology at the largest pediatric hospital in AZ. The clinical voice on whether PM2.5 exposure differentials inside AZ schools are a real respiratory risk to children.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Cardiopulmonary Exercise Testing in Children" in Indian Pediatr on exercise-induced dyspnea and pediatric chronic conditions.

name: James V. Woodward, MD
role: Director, Airway Program; Co-Director, Severe Asthma Clinic
story_relevance_note: Severe Asthma Clinic co-director. He sees the AZ kids whose asthma is hardest to control. Direct angle: do PM2.5 spikes inside school HVAC explain difficult-to-control cases?

name: Craig Harris
story_relevance_note: Beats school district accountability and ESA / public funds. PRR-driven HVAC invoice story is squarely his territory; concrete documents + named pediatric experts + statute citations make this turnkey for his I-team.
personalization_hook: Saw your reporting on 20% unallowable ESA purchases in 12News.

name: Phoenix ABC15 Investigators
story_relevance_note: ABC15 Investigators is the standing tip-line address for the I-Team. Story checks every box: district money, child health, public records, named experts.
personalization_hook: Saw your phone bank with Dave Biscobing and Melissa Blasius taking viewer calls to solve community problems.

name: AZFamily Investigates
story_relevance_note: Standing investigates@ tip line. CBS5 Investigates routinely runs school district money + safety stories.
personalization_hook: Saw your investigation into Mesa Public Schools' $1.2 million spending scandal on AZFamily 3TV last month.

name: Fox 10 Phoenix Newsroom
story_relevance_note: Standing newsroom tip line. Phoenix-area broadcast confirmation candidate; story has strong b-roll potential (filter housings, particle counters).
personalization_hook: Saw your piece on Maricopa County Su.

name: Rupali Drewek, MD
story_relevance_note: Sleep medicine and pulmonology overlap directly with pediatric responses to PM2.5 exposure inside school HVAC. Sleep-disordered breathing in AZ kids living in PM10 non-attainment is the second-order story.

name: Gerald D. Gong, MD
story_relevance_note: Pulmonology physician at the largest AZ pediatric hospital. We need clinical voices on whether PM2.5 differentials inside AZ schools translate to measurable respiratory effects.

name: Andres Herrera Camino, MD
story_relevance_note: Pediatric critical care + pulmonology. Sees the AZ kids landing in PICU for status asthmaticus. Direct hook: do school-day PM2.5 spikes correlate with admissions?

name: Michelle M. Ratkiewicz, DO
story_relevance_note: Pediatric pulmonologist on the team page that confirms AZ's largest children's hospital pulmonology faculty. Story angle: clinical perspective on whether MERV 13 housing failures matter for asthma control.

name: Sophia N. Williams, MD
story_relevance_note: Pediatric pulmonologist at PCH. Clinical voice on whether housing bypass in classrooms is a real risk factor for sensitive children.
personalization_hook: Saw your leadership as Program Director of the Pediatric Pulmonology Fellowship at Phoenix Children's Hospital.

name: Pamela S. Griffiths, MD
story_relevance_note: BPD program director. BPD survivors are exquisitely sensitive to fine particulate exposure. Direct framing: should AZ districts measure PM2.5 because of medically vulnerable children in mainstream classrooms?
personalization_hook: Saw your article "Progress Through Teamwork and the National BPD Collaborative" on advancing BPD treatment at Phoenix Children's.

name: Sharad P. Menon, MD, MBA
story_relevance_note: Pulmonary hypertension clinic director. PM2.5 chronic exposure is a known cardiovascular risk factor (Burnett PNAS 2018, Di NEJM 2017). Pediatric PH cohort is sentinel.
personalization_hook: Saw your comments on Phoenix Children's national recognition as a pediatric pulmonary hy.

name: Albert Dutchover
story_relevance_note: Gilbert is one of AZ's largest unified districts. M&O Director owns HVAC procurement and commissioning decisions. PRR exhibit candidate for filter spec sheets and housing-leakage tests.
personalization_hook: Saw your approval of the $34,147 McQuay Air Conditioning HVAC project estimate with the Arizona School Facilities Board.

name: Bonnie Betz
story_relevance_note: Business Services owns the procurement contracts and ESSER spending records on HVAC upgrades. Direct PRR audience for invoices showing what GPS bought vs. what ASHRAE 241 would require.
personalization_hook: Saw your presentation on the proposed $136M bond request, including $65M for facility upgrades, in AZBEX.

name: Sen. Lela Alston
story_relevance_note: Democratic ranking member on Senate Ed. Long-tenured former educator. LD5 covers central Phoenix Union HSD territory. Likely friendly to a school IAQ disclosure bill.
personalization_hook: Saw your sponsorship of SCR1037 on schools; English language learners; requirements.

name: Andy Linton
story_relevance_note: Director of the agency that runs ambient PM10 and PM2.5 monitoring across the EPA serious non-attainment zone. Direct counterpoint source: ambient readings vs. classroom indoor readings. Statewide regulatory voice.
personalization_hook: Saw your presentation on proposed revisions to Rule 230 (General Permits) at the April 27, 2026 Board of Health meeting.

name: Robert Stratman
story_relevance_note: Deputy Director, Maricopa Air Quality. Operational lead on monitoring and compliance in the PM10 non-attainment zone. Schools sit downwind of multiple monitoring stations.
personalization_hook: Saw your proposal to revise Rule 230 for state compliance at the Maricopa County Board of Health meeting.

name: Sylvie Donaldson
story_relevance_note: Department administrator. Single point of contact on records, technical reports, and monitoring data referenced in the investigation.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on the Maricopa County Air Quality Department's Rule 230 revision proposal to ensure state compliance.

name: Jean Andino, PhD
outlet: ASU Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering
story_relevance_note: ASU professor with an active EPA-funded indoor air quality research program covering classrooms and homes. Direct technical authority: she can speak to whether MERV 13 media without housing seal delivers any measurable PM2.5 reduction inside schools.
personalization_hook: Saw your $1.9M EPA grant for wildfire smoke preparedness and indoor air pollutant control in Arizona classrooms and homes.

name: Pierre Herckes, PhD
story_relevance_note: Aerosol chemistry researcher at ASU; ABOR-funded work on local AZ air pollution sources. Source on what's actually in PM2.5 over Phoenix schools and why media-only filtration misses it.
personalization_hook: Saw your ABOR-funded research on measuring harmful airborne species like ozone and dust to identify local Arizona air pollution.

name: Matt Fraser, PhD
outlet: ASU School of Sustainable Engineering and the Built Environment
story_relevance_note: Built environment researcher with AZ air pollution funding history. Direct authority on whether building HVAC systems as commissioned in AZ districts actually deliver design-spec performance.
personalization_hook: Saw your 2022 Arizona Board of Regents-funded research on local air pollution.

name: Maria Polletta
story_relevance_note: AZCIR's education investigative reporter; previously covered state government for Arizona Republic. PRR-driven school facilities accountability with named clinical experts is exactly her lane.
personalization_hook: Saw your recent work as managing editor overseeing AZCIR's education beat investigations.

name: Natalya Azadeh, MD, MS
story_relevance_note: Mayo AZ pulmonary and critical care faculty. Adult ICU voice on PM2.5 cardiopulmonary risk (Di 2017, Burnett 2018). Mayo's Phoenix campus serves the same air shed.

story_relevance_note: Mayo AZ pulmonologist. Story angle: what PM2.5 does to existing pulmonary patients living in the same air shed as the kids. Clinical-policy bridge.
